title: episki Profile Definition (Control Selection / Tailoring)
description: A profile selects and tailors controls from one or more catalogs (frameworks) for a specific context — e.g. a PCI SAQ type or a CCM-lite subset. Modeled on the OSCAL Profile model: each import points at a catalog and includes/excludes controls by ref. Selection lives here, never inside the catalog.

key.description: Stable slug identifier for the profile. Lowercase, hyphen-separated.

source_url.description: Canonical public reference URL for the profile/selection.

subset_code.description: Short, stable membership label for this selection, e.g. 'A-EP' or 'lite'. This is the token a loader records against each included control (catalog.controls.subsets) and that consumers filter on. Distinct from `key`, which is the slug identifier.

imports.description: Catalogs this profile draws from. Each import selects controls from exactly one catalog.

import.description: A selection from one catalog. Provide either include_all (optionally narrowed by exclude_controls) or an explicit include_controls list.

catalog.description: Key of the catalog framework this import selects from (must match a frameworks/*.json `key`).

include_all.description: Include every control in the catalog. Typically combined with exclude_controls.

include_controls.description: Control refs to include, as written in the catalog (e.g. '1.1.1', 'CC6.1').

exclude_controls.description: Control refs to remove from the selection (meaningful with include_all).
